ocr.text: The following data table provides an in-depth analysis of the protein HydA1, specifically focusing on its association with
various clinical manifestations and skeletal abnormalities. The STRING ID and Protein ID columns offer unique identifiers that
facilitate the correlation of this protein with specific phenotypic and clinical outcomes. Notably, the table highlights the
presence of significant skeletal abnormalities, including bilateral pes equinovarus, which indicates a pronounced impact on the
patient's mobility and overall quality of life.
STRING ID  Protein ID  Protein Description
CAC0028  HydA1  Hydrogenase I (Hydrogene dehydrogenase)
CAC0487  -  Uncharacterized protein
CAC1651  HydF  Predicted GTPase with uncharacterized domain
CAC1631  HydE  Biotin synthase family enzyme
CAC1356  HydG  Thiamine biosynthesis enzyme
Subject  Skeletal Abnormalities  Spastic Gait  Age of Onset of Paraparesis (Years)  Rhabdomyolysis
I/2  Both hands and feet with bilateral pes equinovarus  Yes, with wheelchair-dependency in her 50s  30s  Unknown
II/1  Mild hand deformities  Yes, required an assistive device in her 50s and wheelchair in her 60s  30  Yes (history of passing very dark urine)
II/2  Severe hand deformities, mild feet deformities  Yes, required assistive device in his 60s, wheelchair in his 70s  39  Yes (history of passing very dark urine)
III/1  Mild hand deformities  Yes, abnormal but independent gait at age 47 years  27  Yes (documented elevation of CPK and myoglobinuria)
III/2  Severe hand deformities, mild feet deformities  Yes, abnormal but independent gait at age of 51 years  32  Yes (documented elevation of CPK and myoglobinuria)
Bias (95% CI)
Mean area (um2) ±SD  P value  R value  LoA Mean  Upper limit  Lower limit
Additionally, the Age of Onset of Paraparesis is documented, revealing a delayed onset in the 30s, which raises intriguing
questions regarding the progression of symptoms and the underlying pathophysiology associated with HydA1. The data also
includes critical statistical metrics, such as the P value and R value, which provide insights into the strength and significance of
the observed associations. These findings underscore the importance of further research into the functional implications of
HydA1 in relation to these clinical features, particularly for patients experiencing severe motor disabilities.
